sources.name: CEQ EIS Timeline Report
sources.limitation: EIS only — does not cover EAs or CEs, which represent 99%+ of NEPA actions

sources.name: How Long Does It Take?
sources.qualityNote: Peer-reviewed, project-level data from primary sources
sources.limitation: Small sample sizes, clean energy only

sources.name: Taking Green Energy Projects to Court
Understanding NEPA Litigation
sources.qualityNote: 1,800+ cases with Holland & Knight partnership, systematic coding
sources.limitation: Litigation lens only — not representative of all NEPA reviews

sources.name: Multiple NEPA Reports
sources.qualityNote: Transparent methodology, draws from CEQ/DOE data

sources.name: NEPA Litigation Over Large Energy and Transport Infrastructure Projects
The Cost of Highway Construction
sources.limitation: Shows correlation (post-NEPA cost increase), not proven causation

sources.name: Beyond NEPA: Understanding Complexities of Slow Transmission Buildout
sources.qualityNote: Important counterpoint — 40+ papers reviewed, argues NEPA itself is not primary delay driver
sources.limitation: Small sample (37 projects), transmission sector only

sources.name: $8 Billion a Year: The Cost of America's Broken Permitting System
sources.org: National Association of Manufacturers / Families for Affordable Infrastructure
sources.limitation: Covers ALL federal permitting, not NEPA-specific. Industry-funded advocacy perspective

sources.name: GAO-14-369: Little Information Exists on NEPA Analyses
NEPA Timelines for Clean Energy Projects
sources.qualityNote: 89 clean energy projects with public Google Sheets database

sources.name: How NEPA Works
sources.qualityNote: Best synthesis of cost escalation narrative
